title: Witch Week - Chapter 6 (Fragment 4)
text:
By this time, both of them were shivering. It was cold in the playroom. Charles wondered how he could make Brian go back to bed without going himself. He could not think of a way. So they both went on crouching face to face in the middle of the concrete floor, until there was a sudden little pattering outside the cracked door. Both of them jumped.

“Caretaker’s dog,” whispered Charles.

Brian giggled. “Stupid creature. It looks just like Theresa Mullett’s knitting.”

Charles, before he could stop himself, gave a shriek of laughter. “It does! It does!”

“Shut up!” hissed Brian. “The caretaker’s coming!”

Sure enough, the cracked glass of the door was showing misty torchlight. The dog began yapping furiously on the other side of it. It knew they were there.

Brian and Charles sprang up and fled, through the playroom and out its other door. As it thumped shut behind them, the cracked door thumped open and the hollow playroom echoed with the dog’s little thunderclaps. Without a word, Charles ran one way and Brian ran another. Where Brian went, Charles never knew. He heard the second door thump open as he ran, and the patter of tiny feet behind him. Charles held his glasses on and ran desperately. It was just like the seniors in the shrubbery. What made everyone chase him? Did he smell of witch, or something?

He found an outside door, but it was locked. He pelted on. Behind him, in the distance, he could hear the caretaker bawling to his dog to come back. That made the dog hesitate. Charles, quite terrified by now, put on a spurt and hurled himself through the next door he came to.

There was a feeling of large cold space inside this door. Charles went forward a few cautious steps and hit his foot with a clang on a row of steel chairs. He stood frozen, waiting to be discovered. He could hardly hear for the blood banging in his ears at first. Then he found he could hear the dog yapping again, somewhere quite far off. It seemed to have lost him. At the same time, he found he could see the faint shapes of huge windows, high up, beyond the chairs. He was in the school hall.

It came to Charles that he was not going to get a better opportunity than this. Better summon up his shoes at once. No—forget the shoes. Mr. Wentworth was far more urgent. Get Mr. Wentworth, and when Mr. Wentworth appeared, perhaps Charles could put in a word about Brian.

It was at this point that Charles realized that he dared not fetch Mr. Wentworth back. If Mr. Wentworth did not know who had made him vanish, he would know as soon as he arrived back and found Charles.

“Flaming witches!” Charles moaned. “Why didn’t I think?”

The dog, not too distant, gave another yap. Hunted and undecided, Charles shuffled forward and fell across more chairs. He was in a perfect maze of chairs. He stood where he was and tried to think.

He could still get the shoes, he thought. He could say he was sleepwalking with worry about them when the caretaker found him. Uncertainly, he held up both arms. That dog was definitely coming nearer again.

“Shoes,” Charles said hurriedly, and his voice cracked with fear and cold and lack of breathe. “Shoes. Come to me. Hey presto. Abracadabra. Shoes, I say!” The dog sounded almost outside the hall door now. Charles made dragging movements with his hands and then crossed them over his chest. “Shoes!”

vocabulary.definition: to shout or call loudly
vocabulary.example: Behind him, in the distance, he could hear the caretaker bawling to his dog to come back.

vocabulary.definition: careful to avoid problems or danger
vocabulary.example: Charles went forward a few cautious steps and hit his foot with a clang on a row of steel chairs.

vocabulary.definition: a loud, resonant metallic sound
vocabulary.example: Charles went forward a few cautious steps and hit his foot with a clang on a row of steel chairs.

vocabulary.definition: a hard building material made by mixing together cement, sand, small stones, and water
vocabulary.example: So they both went on crouching face to face in the middle of the concrete floor, until there was a sudden little pattering outside the cracked door.

vocabulary.definition: broken without being separated into pieces
vocabulary.example: So they both went on crouching face to face in the middle of the concrete floor, until there was a sudden little pattering outside the cracked door.

vocabulary.definition: to bend your knees and lower yourself so that you are close to the ground
vocabulary.example: So they both went on crouching face to face in the middle of the concrete floor, until there was a sudden little pattering outside the cracked door.

vocabulary.definition: in a way that shows great urgency or despair
vocabulary.example: Charles held his glasses on and ran desperately.

vocabulary.definition: to produce a sound that is repeated
vocabulary.example: As it thumped shut behind them, the cracked door thumped open and the hollow playroom echoed with the dog’s little thunderclaps.

vocabulary.definition: barely perceptible or weak
vocabulary.example: At the same time, he found he could see the faint shapes of huge windows, high up, beyond the chairs.

vocabulary.definition: in a very angry, fast, or intense way
vocabulary.example: The dog began yapping furiously on the other side of it.

vocabulary.definition: to pause before doing something because you are uncertain or nervous
vocabulary.example: That made the dog hesitate.

vocabulary.definition: to make a sharp sibilant sound like the letter 's', often to express anger or to tell someone to be quiet
vocabulary.example: ” hissed Brian.

vocabulary.definition: chased or pursued as prey or target
vocabulary.example: Hunted and undecided, Charles shuffled forward and fell across more chairs.

vocabulary.definition: a network of paths or passages that is difficult to find your way through
vocabulary.example: He was in a perfect maze of chairs.

vocabulary.definition: filled or covered with mist; not clear
vocabulary.example:
”

Sure enough, the cracked glass of the door was showing misty torchlight.

vocabulary.definition: to make a long, low sound expressing pain, sadness, or displeasure
vocabulary.example: ” Charles moaned.

vocabulary.definition: to make quick, light tapping sounds
vocabulary.example: So they both went on crouching face to face in the middle of the concrete floor, until there was a sudden little pattering outside the cracked door.

vocabulary.definition: to shake slightly because you are cold, frightened, or ill
vocabulary.example: By this time, both of them were shivering.

vocabulary.definition: a loud, high-pitched cry expressing pain, fear, or surprise
vocabulary.example:
”

Charles, before he could stop himself, gave a shriek of laughter.

vocabulary.definition: to walk by dragging your feet
vocabulary.example: Hunted and undecided, Charles shuffled forward and fell across more chairs.

vocabulary.definition: to move quickly and suddenly
vocabulary.example: Brian and Charles sprang up and fled, through the playroom and out its other door.

vocabulary.definition: extremely scared or afraid
vocabulary.example: Charles, quite terrified by now, put on a spurt and hurled himself through the next door he came to.

vocabulary.definition: not having made a decision
vocabulary.example: Hunted and undecided, Charles shuffled forward and fell across more chairs.

vocabulary.definition: needing to be dealt with immediately
vocabulary.example: Wentworth was far more urgent.

vocabulary.definition: to disappear suddenly and completely
vocabulary.example: Wentworth did not know who had made him vanish, he would know as soon as he arrived back and found Charles.

vocabulary.definition: making quick, sharp barks, usually like a small dog
vocabulary.example: The dog began yapping furiously on the other side of it.
